behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is wiring across CSV parsing, schema validation, and DLQ emission (including row-number propagation). A unit test of just the validator can miss real mapping/routing bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline on a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields and mixed valid rows, using real parser+validator+DLQ modules wired together (no mocked validator/DLQ contract). Assert rejected rows are in DLQ with exact original CSV row numbers and valid rows continue through normal path.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is parsing/normalization logic inside transformation code. No I/O or external runtime behavior is required to expose incorrect format handling.
behaviors.test_description: Test the date-normalization function directly with representative ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (plus edge cases like ambiguous dates), asserting canonical output and deterministic timezone handling.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on runtime memory behavior, throughput, and timeout enforcement under load, which are OS/process-level characteristics that mocks cannot faithfully reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500,000-row CSV and real configured timeout settings; capture wall-clock duration and peak RSS memory. Fail if processing exceeds timeout or crosses memory budget / OOMs.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often occurs at HTTP/client boundary (status-to-exception mapping, retryability flags, backoff timing). Pure in-process mocks can hide these behaviors and falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an S3-compatible endpoint or controllable HTTP stub that returns 503 for initial requests then succeeds. Verify actual retry attempts, increasing backoff intervals, and eventual batch success without full-batch abort.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This is a crash/restart durability/idempotency boundary involving process lifecycle plus real DB state. Mocked DB or same-process simulation can miss transaction/commit and restart edge failures.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline against real PostgreSQL, force termination mid-batch after some commits, then restart with same input/checkpoint state. Assert final table has exactly one copy per logical row (no duplicates) and expected total count.
